title: Korean-Style Pork Wrap
description: A flavorful and spicy Korean-inspired wrap filled with marinated pork, sauerkraut, and crunchy slaw.
instructions: 1. In a large bowl, whisk together chili flakes, sesame oil, and 2 tablespoons of water to make the marinade.
2. Add the pork shoulder to the marinade and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
3. Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Remove the pork from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
4. Grill the pork for 5-7 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.
5. Let the pork rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
6. Warm the tortilla by wrapping it in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
7. Assemble the wrap by spreading a layer of sauerkraut on the tortilla, followed by sliced pork, chopped kimchi, cucumber, green onion, and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.
8. Top with crunchy slaw and serve immediately.
